vulnerability.reference: Over-dependence on automated guard models without robust human oversight, driven by cost and scalability pressures.
vulnerability.generated: Guard models rely on surface-level pattern detection, lacking robustness to detect stealthy, controllable adversarial suffixes that mimic benign text.

impact.reference: Unfettered generation of toxic content, leading to user harm, legal liability, and undermined safety claims.
impact.generated: End-users receive unfiltered harmful LLM outputs, bypassing safety filters and enabling large-scale policy violations and real-world harms.

dose_response.reference: Adversarial suffixes circumvent guard models, allowing toxic content to reach users unfiltered due to over-dependence on automation, resulting in user harm and legal liability.
dose_response.generated: Stealthy, controllable generation of adversarial suffixes, through guard model components, triggers reliance on surface-level pattern detection that misses such mimics, leading to unfiltered harmful LLM outputs, bypassing safety filters and enabling large-scale policy violations and real-world harms.
